data.title: Danone ADR Non-GAAP EPS of €3.80, revenue of €27.28B; introduces FY26 outlook
data.description:
• Danone ADR press release (DANOY): FY Non-GAAP EPS of €3.80.
  • Revenue of €27.28B (-0.4% Y/Y).
  • €2.8bn Free cash flow, reflecting strong operating performance.
  • Proposed dividend up +4.7% to €2.25.
  • 2026 guidance in line with mid-term ambition: like-for-like sales growth expected between +3% and +5%, with recurring operating income growing faster than sales

data.title: Tandem Diabetes Care targets 10-11% pump shipment growth in 2026 with PayGo transition
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Tandem Diabetes Care, Inc. (TNDM) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO John Sheridan stated, "2025 was a defining year for Tandem, where we surpassed the milestone of $1 billion in sales while delivering on our mission to provide new innovations, improved outcomes and a revolutionary experience to nearly 0.5 million customers worldwide." He highlighted record-setting Q4 results, double-digit growth, and improved profitability. Sheridan emphasized three main initiatives: modernizing commercial operations, delivering new technology, and reshaping the business model. Direct commercial operations launched in the U.K., Switzerland, and Austria, with more European expansion planned for 2026 and 2027.
  • Sheridan outlined technology advances, including the launch of Control-IQ+, new features for t:slim and Mobi, and upcoming launches of Mobi internationally and new sensor integrations. He explained, "We plan to build on this momentum in 2026 and have multiple new products either imminently launching, awaiting regulatory clearance or reaching key milestones in the development path."
  • Sheridan described a strategic shift: "A key aspect of this acceleration is our decision to adopt a pay-as-you-go reimbursement structure, which creates a near-term offset to sales while significantly strengthening our business model over time."
  • CFO Leigh Vosseller said, "2025 was a record year for Tandem, which is highlighted by our milestone achievement of more than $1 billion in worldwide sales and multiple records in Q4, including our highest sales, gross margin and pump shipments." She further noted, "We delivered on our commitment to improving profitability in a meaningful way. We expanded gross margin by 3 percentage points to 54% for the full year and reported our highest quarterly margin ever at 58%."

Outlook

  • Vosseller announced, "Our 2026 U.S. sales are expected to be in the range of $730 million to $745 million based on growth in pump shipments of 10% to 11% year-over-year. This incorporates $70 million to $80 million of pricing headwinds, reflecting our adoption of a pay-as-you-go model."
  • "Pump shipments in the U.S. are expected to increase 10% to 11% year-over-year, returning to new pump growth led by MDI conversions. Renewal pumps are expected to comprise more than half of total shipments."
  • "International sales are expected to be in the range of $335 million to $340 million for the year."
  • "Worldwide sales for 2026 are expected to be in the range of $1.065 billion to $1.085 billion. This incorporates $85 million to $95 million in total sales headwinds associated with our strategic business model changes."
  • Vosseller projected gross margin to "step up to a range of 56% to 57%, scaling from nearly 54% in the first quarter to 60% in the fourth quarter. Adjusted EBITDA is also expected to demonstrate leverage in the range of 5% to 6% for the full year of 2026."

Financial Results

  • Vosseller reported, "In 2025, worldwide sales grew 12%, our second year in a row of double-digit sales growth based on a 10% increase in the U.S. to $707 million and 15% internationally to $308 million."
  • "Our record worldwide sales of $290 million represented 15% year-over-year growth. This is the strongest sales quarter in our company's history."
  • U.S. Q4 sales increased 14% to $210 million, driven by more than 27,000 pump shipments. Pharmacy channel sales nearly doubled from Q3, growing to $16 million or 7% of total U.S. sales.
  • Internationally, Q4 sales reached $80 million, up 17% year-over-year with 11,000 pump shipments. "We also realized benefit from favorable FX, offset by $4 million associated with our transition to direct operations, primarily impacting pump sales."
  • Vosseller highlighted, "Adjusted EBITDA was 11% of sales in the fourth quarter, a 10 percentage point improvement over the prior year. Additionally, we generated our first positive operating margin since 2021 at 3% of sales in Q4."

Q&A

  • Matthew Miksic, Barclays: Asked about the underlying growth excluding PayGo changes. Sheridan: "I think if you look at the overall revenue growth or shipment growth for the year, it's going to be in the line of 10% to 11%. So it's going to be double-digit growth in shipments."
  • Mathew Blackman, TD Cowen: Inquired about pharmacy coverage and contracting progress. Vosseller: "We do have contracts with all of the major PBMs, the top 3, which gets you about 80% of covered lives under contract. But what we're really focused on is the formulary access where we have roughly 1/3 of lives covered today."
  • Larry Biegelsen, Wells Fargo: Asked why now for PayGo and long-term pharmacy goals. Sheridan: "We've talked about pharmacy for a while now. And I think it's absolutely the right time to make this transition."
  • Chris Pasquale, Nephron Research: Asked about the international transition timeline. Sheridan: "We're basically using that as a playbook now, and we're going to do the exact same thing this year for the next countries that come in 2027."
  • Danielle Antalffy, UBS: Asked about margin levers. Vosseller: "One of them really comes from Mobi as we continue to build and scale that part of the business. In the long term, Mobi...the manufacturing cost of a Mobi versus the t:slim is 10% to 15% lower."
  • Matthew O'Brien, Piper Sandler: Asked about confidence in new pump shipment growth. Sheridan: "We have high confidence we're going to get this thing approved this year."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts pressed for clarity on PayGo implementation, international expansion, and gross margin progression, with a neutral to slightly positive tone, often congratulating management but seeking specifics on risks and timing.
  • Management maintained a confident and optimistic tone in both prepared remarks and Q&A, frequently using phrases like "we are excited about it," "we feel highly confident," and "we look forward to demonstrating those in the coming quarters." In Q&A, management was occasionally more detailed and explanatory, particularly on operational logistics and transition challenges.
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analyst sentiment were more focused on execution and transition risks, though overall confidence in long-term prospects remained strong.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • The current quarter featured explicit guidance for 2026, including detailed figures for sales, margins, and pump shipment growth, while the previous quarter stopped short of explicit guidance and focused on setting expectations.
  • The PayGo transition was announced as a major strategic shift, compared to prior incremental pharmacy expansion. Management now targets a majority of shipments through pharmacy in 2-3 years, versus earlier measured steps.
  • Analysts in the current call focused heavily on transition execution, P&L impacts, and international direct operations, compared to prior quarter's questions about pricing uplift and renewal trends.
  • Management's confidence in margin expansion and pipeline strength was consistent, but more forward-looking detail on business model evolution was provided this quarter.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management cited pricing headwinds from the PayGo transition, which are expected to moderate revenue growth in the near term.
  • The transition to direct operations internationally presents short-term sales headwinds from distributor destocking and inventory buybacks.
  • Analysts expressed concerns about the timing and execution of PayGo, attrition rates, and the pace of international direct model adoption.
  • Management described mitigation strategies, including leveraging the large installed base to offset revenue impact, expanding formulary access, and scaling pharmacy and direct operations gradually.

Final Takeaway

Tandem Diabetes Care closed 2025 with record-breaking results and outlined a transformative shift to a pay-as-you-go business model, targeting 10-11% pump shipment growth in 2026 and further expansion of pharmacy and international direct operations. Management emphasized that while near-term revenue and margin headwinds are expected during this transition, long-term value creation, enhanced profitability, and accelerated growth are anticipated as the new model scales and new innovations reach the market.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: L'Air Liquide S.A. GAAP EPS of €6.10, revenue of €26.94B; sets FY26 targets
data.description:
• L'Air Liquide S.A. press release (AIQUF): FY GAAP EPS of €6.10.
  • Revenue of €26.94B (-0.4% Y/Y).
  • Recurring ROCE increased to 11.2%.
  • Backlog now stands at €4.9 billion.
  • At the Annual General Meeting on May 5, 2026, the payment of a dividend of 3.70 euros per share will be proposed to shareholders for the fiscal year 2025. The proposed dividend is up +12.1% compared to the previous year and up +38% over the last 3 years.
  • Outlook: In 2026, Air Liquide is very confident in its ability to increase its operating margin by +100 basis points and to deliver recurring net profit growth, at constant exchange rates. To provide enhanced visibility on its long-term trajectory, Air Liquide is raising and extending its margin expansion ambition, demonstrating the Group's confidence in its operational excellence:Confirmation of the 2026 objective for operating margin improvement of +100 basis points, excluding the energy impact;
  • Introduction of a new objective for 2027 to improve the operating margin by +100 basis points, excluding the energy impact;
  • The cumulative ambition thus stands at +560 basis points over the six-year period, 2022-2027.
  • To outline the levers of this long-term financial trajectory, the Group will host a Capital Markets Day in H2 2026

data.title: UK retail sales rise 4.5% in January
data.description:
• Retail sales in the United Kingdom increased 4.50 percent in January of 2026 over the same month in the previous year, beating concensus of 2.8% growth.
  • Retail sales in the United Kingdom increased 1.80 percent in January of 2026 over the previous month. 

  • ETFs: (NYSEARCA:FXB), (NYSEARCA:EWU), (NASDAQ:FKU), (BATS:EWUS), (NYSEARCA:FLGB)
  • Currency: (GBP:USD)

data.description:

  • Revenue of $46.98M (-0.3% Y/Y).

data.title: Moncler reports FY results
data.description:
• Moncler press release (MONRF): FY Revenue of Є3.13B (+0.6% Y/Y).
  • MONCLER REVENUES: Є2,720.9 million in 2025, up 3% cFX (+1% at current exchange rates) compared with Є2,707.3 million in 2024
  • STONE ISLAND REVENUES: Є411.2 million in 2025, up 4% cFX (+2% at current exchange rates) compared with Є401.6 million in 2024.
  • GROUP EBIT: Є913.4 million in 2025 compared with Є916.3 million in the previous year. EBIT margin of 29.2% vs 29.5% in 2024, showing resilience despite a more challenging trading environment.

data.title: Australia’s manufacturing and services growth moderates in February
data.description:
The S&P Global Australia Manufacturing PMI decreased to 51.5 in February 2026 from 52.3 in January, indicating slower expansion.

The S&P Global Flash Australia Services PMI also dropped to 52.2 in February from 56.3 in January, showing softer expansion. The Composite PMI fell to 52.0, marking continued but slower growth for the seventeenth month.

The S&P/ASX 200 Index fell 0.05% to around 9,060 in early Friday deals, snapping a four-session winning streak, weighed down by losses in heavyweight miner Rio Tinto. The Australian dollar slipped to below $0.703 on Friday, heading for its first weekly loss in five weeks, pressured by a stronger US dollar and softer domestic PMI figures.

More on Australia:

data.title: SPAR Group CEO Angelo Swartz resigns; CFO Reeza Isaacs named successor
data.description:
• South African retailer SPAR Group Ltd. (SGPPF) on Friday said that Angelo Swartz will step down as CEO and executive director on February 28, with current CFO Reeza Isaacs appointed as his successor effective March 1, 2026.
  • Isaacs has served as CFO and, over the past year, has played a pivotal role in strengthening the group's financial position and reinforcing capital discipline, the company said.
  • Megan Pydigadu, the current COO, has been appointed group CFO with effect from March 1.

data.title: Asia stocks slump as U.S.-Iran conflict looms; South Korea’s KOSPI hits record high
data.description:
Asian stocks traded mostly lower on Friday amid a U.S. military buildup in the Middle East and a sharp selloff on Wall Street overnight. However, South Korea's KOSPI rose to new highs, bolstered by recent optimism in technology.

Gold hovered around $5,000 per ounce on Friday, holding a two-day gain as traders weighed geopolitical risks alongside the Federal Reserve’s policy outlook.

Malaysia’s trade surplus jumped to MYR 21.37 billion in January 2026 from MYR 3.66 billion a year earlier, far surpassing market expectations of MYR 10.1 billion. Exports rose 19.6% year-on-year, and imports rose 5.3% yoy to MYR 125.50 billion in January 2026.

The benchmark KOSPI rose more than 1% to around 5,750 on Friday, extending its record-breaking rally as strength in industrial and defense shares offset mild profit-taking in semiconductor heavyweights.

Japan (NKY:IND) fell 1.07% to around 56,900, while the broader Topix Index slid 1.2% to 3,805 on Friday, breaking a two-day rally, with tech and bank stocks leading the retreat. The Japanese yen slipped past 155 per dollar on Friday, falling for the third consecutive session as both headline and core inflation rates slowed in January.

Japan’s annual inflation rate decreased to 1.5% in January 2026 from 2.1% the previous month, marking the lowest level since March 2022. Food inflation also dropped to 3.9%, a 15-month low due to the slowest increase in rice prices in 18 months. Core inflation fell to 2.0%, the lowest since January 2024, staying within the central bank’s 2% target. 

The S&P Global Japan Manufacturing PMI increased to 52.8 in February 2026 from 51.5 in January, indicating strong growth supported by domestic and external demand. The S&P Global Services PMI rose slightly to 53.8 from 53.7, the highest since May 2024, with new orders increasing at a fast rate.

China (SHCOMP) markets closed for the Lunar New Year holiday.

Hong Kong (HSI) fell 0.71% to 26,469 in early Friday trade, reversing gains from the prior session as markets reopened after the Lunar New Year break.

India (SENSEX) rose 0.48% to around 82,493 on Friday, stabilizing after the previous session’s sharp sell-off, though most sectors remained under pressure. 

The HSBC India Manufacturing PMI rose to 57.5 in February 2026 from 55.4 in January, preliminary estimates showed. The HSBC India Services PMI edged down to 58.4 in February 2026 from 58.5 in January.

Australia (AS51) fell 0.05% to around 9,060 in early Friday deals, snapping a four-session winning streak, weighed down by losses in heavyweight miner Rio Tinto. The Australian dollar slipped to below $0.703 on Friday, heading for its first weekly loss in five weeks, pressured by a stronger US dollar and softer domestic PMI figures.

Data showed February flash PMIs cooled across the board, pointing to slower growth but persistent inflation pressures. Composite, services, and manufacturing readings all eased from January, though each remained above 50, signaling continued expansion. 

In the U.S. on Thursday, all three major indexes ended lower as investors pared risk after the FOMC minutes signaled that disinflation may take longer and that higher rates could be needed.

U.S. stock futures were broadly flat on Friday after coming under pressure in the previous session, as investors positioned for key economic releases: Dow +0.22%; S&P 500 +0.33%; Nasdaq +0.37%.

Investors now await PCE data due later today. 

Currencies: (JPY:USD), (CNY:USD), (AUD:USD), (INR:USD), (HKD:USD), (NZD:USD).

More on Asia's markets and economy:

data.title: Novartis to sell 70.7% stake in Indian unit for $159M
data.description:
• Novartis AG (NVS) plans to sell its full 70.68% stake in its publicly listed Indian unit to a private equity-led consortium for around $159 million as part of a wider global restructuring.
  • The consortium, comprising WaveRise Investments, ChrysCapital Fund X, and Two Infinity Partners, will buy the stake in Novartis India through a share purchase agreement.
  • The move comes two years after Novartis began a strategic review of Novartis India, including assessing its stake in the Mumbai-based firm.
